plainEnglish: Anthropic's AI assistant. You give it instructions in plain English; it returns text, code, images, or actions.
cxoAnalogy: An always-on analyst who is fast, literal, and tireless — but needs a clear brief.

plainEnglish: The terminal/IDE app that lets Claude read and edit files on your computer.
cxoAnalogy: Claude with hands. It can open documents, change them, and run things — not just chat.

plainEnglish: The browser experience of working with Claude inside web apps and shared spaces.
cxoAnalogy: Claude in the meeting room — visible to others on your team.

plainEnglish: The instruction you give Claude. The clearer the brief, the better the output.
cxoAnalogy: A brief to a contractor: scope, audience, constraints, success criteria.

plainEnglish: Everything Claude can 'see' in this conversation — your prompt, attached files, and prior messages.
cxoAnalogy: Background materials in a meeting — what Claude already has on the table.

plainEnglish: A specific version of Claude. Bigger models are smarter but slower; smaller ones are faster and cheaper.
cxoAnalogy: Senior vs junior consultant. Use senior for hard things, junior for fast and easy ones.

plainEnglish: Anthropic's most capable Claude model. Use it for complex, multi-step thinking.
cxoAnalogy: Your partner at the firm.

plainEnglish: Anthropic's balanced Claude model. Strong general default for most work.
cxoAnalogy: Your VP — handles the bulk of work, well.

plainEnglish: Anthropic's fastest, cheapest Claude model. Use it for short, simple tasks at high volume.
cxoAnalogy: Your fastest analyst — perfect for triage and quick passes.

plainEnglish: The unit Claude reads and writes in — roughly a word piece. You're billed per token in and out.
cxoAnalogy: Like billable minutes. Pay attention to how many you spend on long inputs.

plainEnglish: Model Context Protocol — a standard that lets Claude talk to other apps (your email, drive, calendar, etc.) through 'MCP servers'.
cxoAnalogy: The USB-C of AI integrations. Plug a new tool in once and any MCP-aware AI can use it.

plainEnglish: A specific MCP server you've enabled, e.g. Gmail or Figma. Connectors give Claude permission to read/write inside that app.
cxoAnalogy: A signed access pass for a specific building.

plainEnglish: A child Claude session you can spin up to handle a piece of work, with its own scratchpad and tools.
cxoAnalogy: Delegating a sub-task to a junior so the partner can keep focus on the big picture.

plainEnglish: A small script Claude Code runs automatically at certain points (before a tool call, after a file edit, etc.).
cxoAnalogy: An office SOP — 'every time X happens, do Y'.

plainEnglish: A shortcut you type into Claude Code starting with '/' to trigger a specific behavior (e.g. /review).
cxoAnalogy: Speed dial. One short keystroke triggers a complex routine.

plainEnglish: A separate working copy of a code project so two things can be edited without stepping on each other.
cxoAnalogy: A second copy of a contract you can mark up without messing up the original.

plainEnglish: A reusable bundle of instructions Claude follows when a relevant task comes up.
cxoAnalogy: A playbook your team can apply when the right situation arises.

plainEnglish: What Claude is allowed to read or do. You can grant or revoke at a granular level.
cxoAnalogy: Role-based access. Just like with employees — give the minimum needed.

plainEnglish: When Claude states something confidently that isn't true. Less common with strong context and verification.
cxoAnalogy: An overconfident new hire winging an answer. Always verify before you ship.

plainEnglish: A dial for randomness. Low = consistent and conservative. High = more creative and varied.
cxoAnalogy: Brainstorm mode vs. board-paper mode.
